ogTitle: Homebush vs Homebush West: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Homebush and Homebush West.
heading: Homebush vs Homebush West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Homebush, New South Wales and Homebush West, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Homebush and Homebush West have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Homebush or Homebush West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Homebush has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Homebush West scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Homebush scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Homebush West scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Homebush or Homebush West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Homebush has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
